title: ADX (Average Directional Index) Indicator
description: Add ADX indicator calculation from price bars. ADX measures trend strength on a 0-100 scale (0-25 weak, 25-50 strong, 50-75 very strong, 75-100 extremely strong). Implement as a new calculator following the existing RSI/MACD/Bollinger pattern, integrate into IndicatorSnapshot, and add as an 8th signal in the recommendation scoring composite.
goal: ADX available via the indicators service, included in IndicatorSnapshot, and contributing to recommendation composite scoring with appropriate weight.
tech_stack: FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy (AsyncSession), PostgreSQL. All I/O must be async.
in_scope: backend/app/indicators/calculators/adx.py — new calculator module with calc_adx() function
backend/app/indicators/service.py — call calc_adx() and add adx_14 field to IndicatorSnapshot
backend/app/indicators/schemas.py — add adx_14: float | None to IndicatorSnapshot schema
backend/app/recommendations/scoring.py — add score_adx() signal extractor and integrate into composite with weight 0.10
backend/tests/test_adx.py — unit tests for ADX calculation and scoring integration

subtasks.title: Implement ADX calculator
subtasks.description:
Create backend/app/indicators/calculators/adx.py with a calc_adx() function.

Signature: def calc_adx(highs: pd.Series, lows: pd.Series, closes: pd.Series, period: int = 14) -> ADXResult | None

ADXResult should be a frozen dataclass with fields: adx (float), plus_di (float), minus_di (float).

Algorithm:
1. Calculate True Range (TR) = max(high-low, |high-prev_close|, |low-prev_close|)
2. Calculate +DM (Plus Directional Movement) = high - prev_high if positive and > (prev_low - low), else 0
3. Calculate -DM (Minus Directional Movement) = prev_low - low if positive and > (high - prev_high), else 0
4. Smooth TR, +DM, -DM using Wilder's smoothing (first value = sum of period, subsequent = prev - prev/period + current)
5. +DI = 100 * smoothed(+DM) / smoothed(TR)
6. -DI = 100 * smoothed(-DM) / smoothed(TR)
7. DX = 100 * |+DI - -DI| / (+DI + -DI)
8. ADX = Wilder's smoothed average of DX over period

Requires minimum 2*period bars (28 for default period=14). Return None if insufficient data.

Also update backend/app/indicators/calculators/__init__.py to export calc_adx and ADXResult.
required_checks: cd backend && uv run python -c "from app.indicators.calculators.adx import calc_adx, ADXResult; print('import OK')"
cd backend && uv run python -m mypy app/indicators/calculators/adx.py --ignore-missing-imports
acceptance_criteria: calc_adx returns ADXResult with adx, plus_di, minus_di fields
Returns None when fewer than 2*period bars provided
ADX value is between 0 and 100 for valid input

risks.explanation: Wilder smoothing must match standard ADX formula; verify with known test values

subtasks.title: Integrate ADX into IndicatorSnapshot and scoring
subtasks.description:
1. In backend/app/indicators/schemas.py: Add adx_14: float | None = None and plus_di: float | None = None and minus_di: float | None = None fields to the IndicatorSnapshot model.

2. In backend/app/indicators/service.py:
   - Import calc_adx from calculators
   - In get_indicators(), extract highs and lows Series from price bars (same pattern as closes)
   - Call calc_adx(highs, lows, closes, period=14)
   - Populate adx_14, plus_di, minus_di in the returned IndicatorSnapshot

3. In backend/app/recommendations/scoring.py:
   - Add score_adx(adx: float | None, plus_di: float | None, minus_di: float | None) -> SignalScore
   - Scoring logic:
     - adx is None → score 0.0 (neutral)
     - adx < 20 → score 0.0 (no trend, no edge)
     - adx 20-25 and +DI > -DI → score +0.3 (emerging bullish trend)
     - adx 20-25 and -DI > +DI → score -0.3 (emerging bearish trend)
     - adx 25-50 and +DI > -DI → score +0.6 (strong bullish trend)
     - adx 25-50 and -DI > +DI → score -0.6 (strong bearish trend)
     - adx > 50 and +DI > -DI → score +0.8 (very strong bull)
     - adx > 50 and -DI > +DI → score -0.8 (very strong bear)
   - Add 'adx' to WEIGHTS dict with weight 0.10. Redistribute by reducing 'price_trend' from 0.20 to 0.15 and 'rsi' from 0.25 to 0.20 so total remains 1.0
   - Include adx signal in compute_recommendation() — accept adx_14, plus_di, minus_di as optional params (default None), call score_adx, add to signals list
required_checks: cd backend && uv run python -m mypy app/indicators/service.py app/indicators/schemas.py app/recommendations/scoring.py --ignore-missing-imports
cd backend && uv run python -c "from app.recommendations.scoring import WEIGHTS; assert abs(sum(WEIGHTS.values()) - 1.0) < 0.001, f'Weights sum to {sum(WEIGHTS.values())}'; print('weights OK')"
acceptance_criteria: IndicatorSnapshot includes adx_14, plus_di, minus_di fields
get_indicators() computes ADX from price bars and populates snapshot
score_adx() returns directional scores based on ADX strength and DI crossover
WEIGHTS dict sums to 1.0 with new 'adx' entry at 0.10
compute_recommendation() accepts and uses ADX parameters

risks.explanation: Changing recommendation weights affects all scoring — weight redistribution must keep total at 1.0 and not dramatically shift existing recommendation classifications

subtasks.title: Unit tests for ADX calculator and scoring
subtasks.description:
Create backend/tests/test_adx.py with these tests:

1. test_adx_insufficient_data — pass < 28 bars, assert returns None
2. test_adx_known_values — create a synthetic trending price series (e.g., steadily rising closes with highs/lows), verify ADX > 25 and +DI > -DI
3. test_adx_range — verify ADX, +DI, -DI are all between 0 and 100
4. test_adx_sideways_market — flat/oscillating prices should produce ADX < 25
5. test_score_adx_none — score_adx(None, None, None) returns score 0.0
6. test_score_adx_strong_bullish — ADX=40, +DI=30, -DI=15 → score +0.6
7. test_score_adx_strong_bearish — ADX=40, +DI=15, -DI=30 → score -0.6
8. test_score_adx_weak_trend — ADX=15 → score 0.0
9. test_weights_sum — verify WEIGHTS sum to 1.0

Use plain pytest (no async needed for pure calculation tests). Import calc_adx from app.indicators.calculators.adx and score_adx from app.recommendations.scoring. Use pd.Series for inputs.
required_checks: cd backend && uv run python -m pytest tests/test_adx.py -q
cd backend && uv run python -m mypy tests/test_adx.py --ignore-missing-imports
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover None/insufficient data edge case
Tests verify scoring logic for bullish, bearish, and weak trend scenarios

risks.explanation: Synthetic price data must be carefully constructed to produce predictable ADX values
